summary: Sum `octaves` of hash-based value noise at frequency*lacunarity^o and amplitude gain^o, amplitude-normalized so the Field1 stays in [-1, 1] regardless of octave count (hash-of-coordinate, not a sequential RNG).

outputs.name: field
outputs.doc: The amplitude-normalized fbm scalar field in [-1, 1].

params.name: width
params.doc: The output field width in pixels.

params.name: height
params.doc: The output field height in pixels.

params.name: frequency
params.doc: Lattice cells per 64 pixels (the base spatial frequency); strictly positive.

params.name: seed
params.doc: The 64-bit seed; the noise is a hash of (coordinate, seed), not a sequential RNG.

params.name: octaves
params.doc: The number of summed octaves, in 1..=32.

params.name: gain
params.doc: The per-octave amplitude multiplier (persistence) in (0, 1]; classically 0.5.

perceptual.reason: field.fbm is an amplitude-normalized sum of hash-based value noise verified by analytic properties (bit-exact reruns, tiling/order invariance, seed sensitivity, octave-count-independent [-1, 1] range via amplitude normalization); there is no perceptual-quality metric to apply
